summary.detailed_summary: The paper proposes a framework for concept-based explainability in EEG transformer models by adapting the TCAV method to identify human-aligned concepts in the latent space. It introduces two ways to define these concepts: using externally labeled EEG datasets and utilizing source localization (eLORETA) to create anatomically defined frequency-band concepts. The authors demonstrate that the BENDR model's internal representations align with known clinical markers for seizures and lateralized cortical activity for motor tasks.
key_points: Method for EEG explainability: Applies Testing Concept Activation Vectors (TCAV) to the BENDR transformer to map latent states to human-understandable concepts.
Novel concept formation: Defines explanatory concepts using both labeled EEG event datasets and anatomically localized cortical activity in specific frequency bands.
Empirical validation: Shows that the model captures epileptiform discharges for seizure detection and lateralization in the somatosensory/motor cortex for motor imagery.
limitations: Coarse spatial resolution of eLORETA makes pinpointing exact cortical sources challenging.
High data attrition rate (90%) when filtering TUH EEG Corpus for reliable resting-state data.
TCAV scores violated normality assumption, requiring non-parametric statistical tests.
Some noise (artifacts) still present in final classifier bottlenecks.

method.architecture: Linear Head BENDR (LHB) consisting of a convolutional feature encoder, transformer, summarizer (adaptive average pooling), and a linear classifier.
method.finetuning: Binary classification for seizure detection and motor movement (left vs right fist).
method.objective: Contrastive self-supervised learning (pre-training) and binary classification (fine-tuning).
method.pretraining: Contrastive self-supervised learning on the TUH EEG Corpus.

summary.one_liner: Applies Concept Activation Vectors (TCAV) to the BENDR transformer model to explain EEG representations using labeled events and anatomically defined brain regions.
summary.title: Concept-based explainability for an EEG transformer model
summary.unique_contribution: Introduces the first TCAV workflow for EEG data, specifically proposing the use of anatomically defined concepts via source localization to interpret deep EEG model representations.

papers.title: Concept-based explainability for an EEG transformer model

reasons: Targets BENDR (2021), which is explicitly described as a large-scale transformer model for EEG.
Focuses on analyzing the representations learned by a deep EEG model using concept-based explainability.
Fits the EEG-FM ecosystem criteria as an analysis of a foundation model's internal latent space.
